message:
feat(webhook+watchdog): Cx-first dispatch_with_cx + join_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Close two production cx gaps in a single slice — both are
detection/lifecycle entry points used by outer supervisor scopes.

1. `WebhookDispatcher::dispatch_with_cx(cx, detection, pane_id,
   rendered, suppressed_since_last)` in webhook.rs — detection-level
   sibling of `dispatch`. Builds the payload synchronously then
   routes through the existing `dispatch_payload_with_cx` so caller
   cx reaches each endpoint's `transport.send_with_cx` call.
   Caller cancellation cuts mid-fanout once the transport observes
   it. Pattern handlers that already hold a cx no longer need to
   construct the payload manually.

2. `WatchdogHandle::join_with_cx(cx)` in watchdog.rs — matches the
   `PaneOutputSubscription::shutdown_with_cx` pattern: signals
   shutdown unconditionally (so the watchdog task always gets a
   chance to exit), then awaits the task handle only if the cx is
   not already cancelled. Cancelled parent scopes bail fast while
   preserving the legacy "signal before return" guarantee.

Legacy `dispatch` + `join` preserved unchanged.

Three regression tests pin the contracts:
- `dispatch_with_cx_routes_through_cx_aware_transport` — records
  that cx-forward dispatch populates `cx_requests` (not `requests`),
  proving the cx reaches the transport.
- `watchdog_join_with_cx_shuts_down_cleanly` — fresh cx signals +
  joins identically to legacy.
- `watchdog_join_with_precancelled_cx_returns_quickly` — pre-cancelled
  cx returns in <500ms even though check_interval is 60s (would
  block indefinitely on legacy `join`).

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features asupersync-runtime): 303/303
webhook + watchdog tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
